steps.title: Identify the grammar rule being tested
steps.content:
This is a pronoun-antecedent agreement question. The pronoun must refer to "the feature-length romance" — a singular noun. The pronoun must be singular to agree.
  • Choice A — ‘it’: Singular pronoun; agrees with singular antecedent "the feature-length romance." ✓
  • Choice B — ‘those’: Plural pronoun; does not agree with singular antecedent. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘these’: Plural pronoun; does not agree with singular antecedent. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘them’: Plural pronoun; does not agree with singular antecedent. ✗

steps.title: Confirm the match
steps.content:
Only "it" is a singular pronoun that correctly refers back to "the feature-length romance." The three other choices are all plural and create a disagreement in number.

That confirms A is correct.
